seed_problem: topological ordering of a task DAG replayed through an exact LRU scratchpad to minimize cache misses
mutation: composite: (goal) turn topological ordering into an LRU-miss-minimizing linear-extension problem; (input) couple DAG precedence with a shared-page structure whose globally-hot pages recur across clusters, so the objective is jointly shaped by dag-precedence, lru-eviction-state, and reuse-distance shaping
theme: compiling a task graph on a tiny scratchpad
title: Compiling a Task Graph on a Tiny Scratchpad
innovation_hook: The schedule does not react to the cache, it authors the reuse-distance profile; the right mental model is shaping a distance histogram under DAG constraints, not chasing locality.
strategies: trivial: emit the given order 1..N (the reference baseline)

greedy: cluster tasks by shared pages -- bucket each task by its rarest (signature) page and emit signature-group by signature-group; cache-oblivious, so globally-hot pages get a whole-cluster reuse distance and are reloaded every cluster (the trap)

strong: cache-state-aware list scheduling that shapes the reuse-distance histogram -- keep globally-hot pages resident by packing their touches, even at the cost of breaking cluster locality; picks the best of several reuse-distance-shaping passes
invalid: emit an out-of-range task id (infeasible) -> score 0
